content: Just as human language learners build accurate vocabulary and expressive writing habits through iterative structured reflection (the classroom sense of reflexion), large language models can improve their self-reflection mechanisms by adopting scaffolded, graded reflection routines modeled on high school language arts writing workshops. This framework would train LLMs to incrementally refine their outputs the same way students learn to revise essays, moving from surface-level word choice checks to deep structural overhauls of their reasoning.
